2013-09-26.abstract: As Indonesians look to their next presidential election, many are talking about a local governor who has demonstrated an unusually common touch with the people.

2013-09-26.title: Sri Lanka: U.N. Official Calls for War Crimes Inquiry
2013-09-26.abstract: The official said she “detected no new or comprehensive effort to independently or credibly investigate the allegations” that civilians were killed by the military.

2013-09-27.title: Italy: Bones Found Amid Shipwreck
2013-09-27.abstract: Divers involved in the recovery of the cruise ship Costa Concordia found several bones on the seabed on Thursday.

2013-09-27.title: As Merkel Is Showered in Glory, Hollande Trudges Through Drizzle
2013-09-27.abstract: President François Hollande of France is struggling at home, while across the border in Germany, Chancellor Angela Merkel is having a much nicer time of it these days.

2013-09-27.title: Chile Hunt for Justice Winds Up as Enigma
2013-09-27.abstract: A former American Navy captain accused of involvement in the murders of two Americans after the 1973  coup may have been right under the noses of the investigators seeking his extradition.

2013-09-27.title: Reporting Iran’s News, but Speaking for One Side
2013-09-27.abstract: In Iran’s news media landscape, which is divided into two camps, the hard-liners and reformists, the semiofficial Fars agency has emerged in recent years as the most influential conservative voice.

2013-09-27.title: Sudan Erupts in Deadly Protests as Gas Prices Rise
2013-09-27.abstract: The police and activists differed on how many Sudanese were killed in protests this week after the government lifted gasoline subsidies, raising the price of gas and other commodities.

2013-09-28.title: Israel: Violent Intifada Anniversary
2013-09-28.abstract: Hundreds of Palestinians clashed with Israeli security forces on Friday as Palestinians marked the 13th anniversary of the outbreak of the second intifada.

2013-09-28.title: Cuba to Let Its Athletes Play Abroad
2013-09-28.abstract: Cuba, a sports-crazed island, has long sought to keep its athletes at home in quasi-amateur leagues, usually prohibiting them from cashing in on multimillion-dollar contracts abroad.

2013-09-28.title: British Leader Is Under Attack From Right and Center
2013-09-28.abstract: Going into this weekend’s Tory conference, Britain’s prime minister, David Cameron, faces a right-wing uprising among legislators who see him as weak on issues like gay marriage and immigration.

2013-09-28.title: A Muslim Prosecutor in Britain, Fighting Forced Marriages and Honor Crimes
2013-09-28.abstract: Nazir Afzal, a Muslim born in England to Pakistani parents, has become London’s go-to lawyer for cases involving violence against women.

2013-09-28.title: Narrow Escapes and Questions on Emergency Response in Attack at Kenya Mall
2013-09-28.abstract: Pretending to be Muslim, hiding in a box and using the blood of a shooting victim were among the ways that potential victims escaped death.

2013-09-29.title: Resignations Threaten Italian Coalition
2013-09-29.abstract: The loss of government ministers from Silvio Berlusconi’s party left open the possibility of a confidence vote or talks on a new coalition.

2013-09-29.title: Former Chilean Intelligence Chief Commits Suicide, Officials Say
2013-09-29.abstract: Gen. Odlanier Mena, 87, shot himself, officials said, after the government announced it would transfer him to a less privileged prison.

2013-09-29.title: Big Challenges Remain Despite Progress on Iran
2013-09-29.abstract: A crucial question is whether the country’s divided leadership is truly willing to dismantle its formidable atomic infrastructure in order to end crippling economic sanctions.

2013-09-29.title: In Damascus, Tension but Also Hope
2013-09-29.abstract: Beneath the surface of what looks like a return to normalcy, the Syrian capital is deeply altered by the country’s civil war, now in its third year.

2013-09-29.title: A Surface Calm, Punctured by Artillery and Weary Arguments
2013-09-29.abstract: Syria’s capital is now a city of checkpoints, new arrivals and a pervasive sense of war weariness, where even those of opposite views seem worn out by their differences.

2013-09-30.title: Amid Nuclear Issue, Israel Said to Arrest Iranian Spy
2013-09-30.abstract: The announcement came as Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu prepared to speak at the United Nations and warned against what he viewed as a deceptive charm offensive by President Hassan Rouhani of Iran.

2013-09-30.title: In Trial, Romania Warily Revisits a Brutal Past
2013-09-30.abstract: The decision to charge a former Ceausescu prison commander has raised hopes among victims and advocates that the country may finally be shaking off a national amnesia about its brutal past.

2013-09-30.title: Qaeda Plot Leak Has Undermined U.S. Intelligence
2013-09-30.abstract: The disclosure in August of a terrorist plot by Al Qaeda has caused more damage to American counterterrorism efforts than the vast trove of data from Edward Snowden, the former N.S.A. consultant, analysts said.

2013-09-30.title: In Romania, a Reckoning Faces Obstacles
2013-09-30.abstract: The decision to charge a former prison commander raises hopes that the country may be able to re-examine a culture of impunity.

2013-09-30.title: Once-Calm Area of Iraq Is Shaken by Bombings
2013-09-30.abstract: After several explosions struck Iraq’s northern Kurdish region, killing at least six people and setting off chaos, a string of car bombs on Monday claimed dozens of lives around Baghdad.
